commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Browser plugin text truncation in CDP and snapshot URL collection paths to strip a dangling high surrogate after fixed-length slicing, with a new colocated Vitest file for the truncation expression.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +48. Total +50 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main uses raw `.slice(0, 100)` and `.slice(0, 120)` on Browser text labels, so astral text at the exact cut boundary can leave a dangling high surrogate. I did not run a live Browser snapshot reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-owned real behavior proof, not a concrete code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.

target.title: fix(browser): truncate element text on code-point boundaries to avoid lone surrogates
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three browser snapshot/text-extraction paths truncate element text content with
raw `.slice(0, N)`. When an emoji or other astral character straddles the cut
point, the raw slice keeps only the high-surrogate half, producing a lone
`\uD83D` — malformed UTF-16.

Affected paths:
- `cdp.ts`: `findCursorInteractiveElements` truncates `textContent` at 100
- `pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts`: `collectSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120
- `routes/agent.snapshot.ts`: `collectChromeMcpSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120

## Why This Change Was Made

Since the affected code runs in the browser page context (CDP `Runtime.evaluate`
/ Playwright `page.evaluate`), the SDK's `truncateUtf16Safe` is not importable.
Instead, `.replace(/[\uD800-\uDBFF]$/, "")` is appended after each `.slice()`
to strip any dangling high surrogate at the cut boundary. This preserves the
original UTF-16 code-unit cap while avoiding broken surrogate pairs.

## User Impact

No user-facing behavior change. Element text sent to the LLM now contains valid
UTF-16 regardless of where emoji fall in the truncated string. The code-unit
caps (100 / 120) remain unchanged.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm test extensions/browser` — 59 tests passed

### Real behavior proof

```
$ node --import tsx proof-browser-text-boundary.mts

=== Truncation with dangling surrogate removal ===
  input: 99x + 😀tail
  slice(0, 100) → 99x + \uD83D        ← lone high surrogate
  after replace → 99x                  ← cleaned

=== Code-unit cap preserved ===
  input: 51x + 25 emoji + tail (101 code units)
  slice(0, 100) → 51x + 24 emoji + \uD83D  ← 100 units, high surrogate at end
  after replace → 51x + 24 emoji            ← 99 units, valid

=== No dangling surrogates ===
  All outputs: ✓ no [\uD800-\uDFFF] in any result
```

Each test exercises the exact `.slice(0, N).replace(/[\uD800-\uDBFF]$/, "")`
pattern used in the three changed paths.

## Tests

`extensions/browser/src/browser/browser-text-truncation.test.ts` — 6 new tests
covering ASCII pass-through, long ASCII truncation, dangling-surrogate removal,
complete-surrogate-pair preservation, empty string, and code-unit cap
preservation.
